She earned a Master of Arts in Near Eastern Languages from Wayne State University in 2011 and later obtained her Juris Doctor from Michigan State University College of Law in 2016.

During law school, Michelle interned at the Michigan Department of the Attorney General in the Civil Litigation, Employment, and Elections Division. She was a Student Tax Clinician in the MSU Alvin L. Storrs Low-Income Taxpayer Clinic and participated in the Tax Clinic’s Alternative Spring Break program. She was also a member of the Journal of Animal and Natural Resource Law.

Before joining Mike Morse Law Firm, Michelle worked at several firms representing insurers and defending no-fault, slip and fall, and dog bite cases. She is passionate about helping others and ensuring clients understand the legal process.

In her free time, Michelle enjoys reading, traveling, and discovering new destinations with her husband.

title: Traffic/Rankings trend
assignees: Alina Neamtu, Anamaria Avram, Andrei Pocol, Anna Polishchuk, Barnes, Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Chris Traynor, Clayton Coombs, Corey V, Daniel, David Nickerson, Den, Dorin Gherman, Emerald Green, Ethan Vandenberg, Evgenii Gusarovskii, Fed, Jason Candido, Jessica Mena, Jim Aiyanyor, Jo-Ann, Joel Nkadimeng, Johan Cilliers, Jordan, Justyna Acosta, Kaylee Hyde, Keith Bruss, Mary De Guzman, Matthew Nyahari, Mikey King, Mubeen Dildar-Mia, Mvelo Nsibanyoni, Predrag, Sadie de Pinto, Savana, Sky Ortigas, Thys du Plooy
description:
I'm more convinced than ever that the March Core update is affecting most, if not all, of our clients' websites.

This is our single most important client, and the trend is very concerning.

My read on the landscape is

A. Our content is largely stale, out of date, or in need of new, fresh insights. For the past several years, we've spent far more of our content efforts on blog posts than on our internal pages, and this update (in my view) is exposing that. I've looked at various pages on some of our clients' sites, and I see oftentimes that we're citing statistics and data from 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, but not 25 or 26. We need a heroic effort to focus on refreshing and updating our clients' most critical pages. We tell clients and prospects that we're data-driven. That we let the data lead the path forward. It's time to live up to that fully.

All the discussions lately about Non-Commodity content aren't a fad. This is what's happening to the whole world of search. Google is on a de-indexing tear.

Steve recently published a resource that could be very helpful https://seonotebook.notion.site/Non-Commodity-Content-Solved-with-a-GPT-35b8c36851918070af13eaa1d61e368d

B. We need to get serious about YMYL standards and begin ensuring our clients' sites

title: Duplicate GCLID in URL Causing Salesforce Attribution Issues
description:
Background

Edward at MMLF reported an issue where two gclid parameters appear in the landing URL, and Salesforce is occasionally capturing the incorrect one. This appears to be affecting lead attribution. 

Example URL from the client: 

https://www.855mikewins.com/settlement-calculator/?campaignid=22119347256&adgroupid=175876955480&creative=792546217124&matchtype=b&network=g&device=c&keyword=rear%20end%20accident&gclid=CjwKCAiAtq_NBhA_EiwA78nNWKuhbFoG7yHVvyQpmSfhguJupc3dAiDfJpYX_IjtpNFgIl9Y15OChBoCVAIQAvD_BwE&wbraid=CkAKCAiAk6rNBhBWEjAAprGgiFuHr8yib5WvPoh8cseUmq1QAVxFpUPWFSPdeb1UIPUKaLXHcJlgKFz-niQaArNl&utm_source=google&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=t11-autoaccident-manual&utm_content=175876955480&utm_term=rear%20end%20accident&hsa_acc=7498865131&hsa_cam=22119347256&hsa_grp=175876955480&hsa_ad=792546217124&hsa_src=g&hsa_tgt=kwd-297128606551&hsa_kw=rear%20end%20accident&hsa_mt=b&hsa_net=adwords&hsa_ver=3&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=22119347256&gbraid=0AAAAAD9DO_tjpjN49JMDCYHqgrL6H3RhH&gclid=CjwKCAiAtq_NBhA_EiwA78nNWKuhbFoG7

Observed Issue

The URL contains two gclid parameters:

  • First GCLID
CjwKCAiAtq_NBhA_EiwA78nNWKuhbFoG7yHVvyQpmSfhguJupc3dAiDfJpYX_IjtpNFgIl9Y15OChBoCVAIQAvD_BwE

  • Second GCLID
CjwKCAiAtq_NBhA_EiwA78nNWKuhbFoG7
 
The second appears to be a truncated version of the first, and Salesforce may be capturing the shorter one instead of the full value.

Questions for Investigation
 
  1. What could cause two gclid parameters to appear in the URL?
  2. How can we ensure Salesforce captures the correct GCLID value when duplicates exist?
  3. Is this something we can help the client with, or do we need to redirect them elsewhere? If so, to where?

title: Trudy's SEO Question
description:
Email from Trudy:

Hi Savana,

We want to create some pages on the website like this. For SEO purposes, would this be better as a blog post (could be one big post or individual posts on each company) or we can create a guide that we put under our Resources section like what we did with the MI No-Fault Guide? We could also do both.  Can you let me know option is optimal for SEO?
Thanks!

Photo:

Corey's response:

Trudy,

Ok, I've had a good chat with Daniel and Clayton over this question. I have a good sense of how to approach it ideally.

The deliberation that I was having where I mentioned possible "downsides" might have been somewhat of an overstatement. A better phrase might have been "implications". Specifically, a page might carry with it the parent page's embedded category. Whereas a post just comes off the blog and doesn't have anything attached to it.

So I wanted a chance to think through it strategically.

I also wanted to look at Stewart's site and see if it seems like all this insurance content has actually helped their SEO. I'm unclear about that. None of those insurance pages are super authoritative, and none of them rank for a lot of the keywords you might imagine those pages would rank for.

But on the other hand he has seen some improvement in the last year on all of his Houston topical silo. Hard for me to really know to what extent those pages might have contributed to that. My guess is not a lot.

That all being said - we have a plan for how to implement this for you. Clayton had to leave for the day for a personal matter, but I'll work with him next week to document the plan and we can discuss it on our call.

So let us take this concept off your plate and run with it. Sound good?

, please outline your thoughts along these lines for Clayton and then tag him in!

In the process, the resource library content went down altogether. I was able to troubleshoot and had them update the CNAME and A records in GoDaddy for fireproofmasterclass.com. The resource library is now live and accessible again.

HOWEVER: fireproofmasterclass.com now redirects to fireproofperformance.com. This is functional, but their ideal situation would be that fpresourcelibrary.com takes users directly to the resource library.

The resource library is configured through Kajabi. Keith has access, and has done some configurations before. In this task: Post Live Redirect, Keith said he implemented a workaround for a previous Kajabi configuration. I'm wondering if either that may be part of the issue we see now, or if he'll have some insight into how to fix this. Can we have him look at this tomorrow morning? I can hop on the dev call around 10:30 MT if I need to, or I can hop on a 1-on-1 with him. 

I am tagging too for visibility. 

Thanks!

title: Fireproof Home Page/Landing Page Project
assignees: Amber Brunett, Corey V, Keith Bruss, Trudy Emlaw
description:
and are working on copy/design for the changes that need to be made.

On our call on Thursday we discussed a framework for getting a V1.0 done by the event this week per Michael's request.

The framework is

  1. BIG IDEA: E.g. "We/I do (specific outcome Fireproof audience wants), Without (specific pain points Fireproof Audience HATES)
  2. ANSWER AUDIENCES NEXT QUESTION: "How do you do that?" (Fireproof Offers Expert Coaching & Masterminds)
  3. ANSWER AUDIENCE NEXT QUESTION: "How Do I Know It Works?" (Show Social Proof/Case Studies/Reviews/Testimonials Etc.)
  4. ANSWER AUDIENCE NEXT QUESTION: "Yes, but My Firm Is Unique/Different because ________" (Firerpoof can help whether you (list exceptions that a prospect might think would make them beyond help e.g. too small, practice something other than PI, don't have a lot of capital, don't have a leadership team, have huge competitors in their market...etc.)

    WE ONLY Have time to focus on 1 and 2 by this week's event. Ideally, we'd need to start making those changes last week, but we're going to want to have something in our developers hands today/tomorrow in order to ensure success.

    Things still needed:

    Access to Kajabi

title: New Post for GMB
description:
Hi Chris,


I want to have a post added to the GMB about the Joel Morse Endowed Scholarship that was just announced yesterday. Below is the description/caption. I’m not sure if you can link to the press release or not. If so, here is a link to the press release. If not, attached is the photo I’d like used in the post.


Thank you!


Trudy



Caption/Description:


We’re proud to announce Mike has donated $25,0000 to set up the Joel Morse Endowed Scholarship for Wayne State University Law students as part of his commitment to racial diversity. This fund is the newest addition to a series of initiatives geared toward giving back to our beloved community. The scholarship is named after Mike’s father, Joel Samuel Morse, who received his undergraduate degree from Wayne State University and was Mike's inspiration to become a lawyer.


“‘To whom much is given, much is required’ was a core value that my dad lived daily as he fought to help people in his law practice and make a difference in his community. It was instilled in me very early that I had a responsibility to not only fight for my clients but to help to make the world around me better,” he says.


Trudy Emlaw | Marketing Director

title: Report Michigan Auto Law
Please post to GMB...
description:
Five years ago, a Detroit woman was driving home from work when another driver ran a stop sign and collided into her. She suffered traumatic injuries to her neck, back, and much of the rest of her body, which caused chronic pain and necessitated several surgeries. When her insurance company failed to cover her medical bills, she called the Mike Morse Law Firm.


The woman’s file was assigned to Jeff Kerby, one of our senior attorneys. Jeff handled her case with diligence and determination, and negotiated a $569,000 settlement, which covered all of her medical expenses. By holding the big insurance companies accountable, Jeff has given his client the peace of mind to move forward with her life. Tremendous work, Jeff!


#MikeMorseLawFirm #855MikeWins #WeAreHereToHelpYou

Poorly maintained roads cause over 50% of car accidents and car fatalities in America.
